name: Canning Corn (Whole Kernel)
recipeIngredient: An average of 31½ pounds (in husks) of sweet corn is needed per canner load of 7 quarts; an average of 20 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints. A bushel weighs 35 pounds and yields 6 to 11 quarts, averaging 4-1/2 pounds per quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Quality: Select ears containing slightly immature kernels or of ideal quality for eating fresh. Canning some sweeter varieties or too immature kernels may cause browning. Check color and flavor before canning large quantities.
2. Procedure:
- Husk corn, remove silk, and wash.
- Blanch 3 minutes in boiling water.
- Cut corn from the cob at about three-fourths the depth of the kernel. Do not scrape the cob.
3. Hot pack: In a saucepan, combine clean quarts of kernels with 1 cup of hot water. Heat to boiling and simmer for 5 minutes. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jar, if desired. Fill jars with corn and cooking liquid, leaving 1-inch headspace.
4. Raw pack: Fill jars with raw kernels, leaving 1-inch headspace. Do not shake or press down. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jar, if desired.
5. Add fresh boiling water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
6. Adjust lids and process as recommended in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Whole Kernel Corn in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot and Raw
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 55 minutes for pints, 85 minutes for quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
Table 2: Recommended process time for Whole Kernel Corn in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot and Raw
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 55 minutes for pints, 85 minutes for quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb. Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
